steps.title: Understand what the underlined phrase does in context
steps.content:
The passage opens with a mongoose example and then introduces the underlined phrase after a colon: intraspecific contact with perceived outsiders. That colon signals that the phrase names the broader concept the mongoose scenario was illustrating. The rest of the text then asks whether this type of interaction drives higher cognition — making the phrase the central subject the whole passage orbits around.
  • Choice A — It establishes that perceiving an individual of the same species as an outsider exemplifies the kind of analysis mentioned later in the text.: The phrase does not exemplify the nuanced analysis mentioned later; it names a type of interaction. ✗
  • Choice B — It identifies a particular type of social interaction whose relevance to higher cognition in animals is the overall focus of the text.: The phrase names intraspecific outsider contact, and the text proceeds to argue this interaction matters comparably to within-group demands for driving cognition — exactly what B says. ✓
  • Choice C — It narrows the text's focus from the full complement of social life dimensions across species to one applicable to only a subset of species.: The passage says this interaction applies across species — it does not restrict the scope to a subset. ✗
  • Choice D — It defines a scientific term central to the text's discussion of the role of complex sociality in driving cognitive adaptations in animals.: The phrase is introduced, not defined; the text never provides a formal definition of the term. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
